title: God - Wikipedia
description: In monotheistic religious belief systems, [1]Godis usually viewed as the supreme being, Creator, Sustainer and principal foundation of faith. [2] In polytheistic belief, agodis "a spirit or being believed to have created, or to control some part of the universe or life, for which such a deity is often worshipped". [3] Belief in the existence of at least one deity, who may interact with the ...

title: The Attributes of God: What They Mean and Why They Matter
description: Learn aboutGod'sself-existence, immutability, self-sufficiency, omnipotence, and other qualities that reveal his glory and majesty. This web page explains each attribute with biblical references and examples, and shows how they matter for our faith and trust inGod.

title: GOD Definition & Meaning - Merriam-Webster
description: The meaning ofGODis the supreme or ultimate reality. How to usegodin a sentence.

title: Who is God? - GotQuestions.org
description: Who isGod? Answer Who isGod? What isGod? How can we knowGod? Who isGod? - The Fact The fact ofGod'sexistence is so conspicuous, both through creation and through man's conscience, that the Bible calls the atheist a "fool" (Psalm 14:1).

title: Who is God? His Nature, Roles, and Works | Christianity.com
description: No other question is more important than "Who isGod?" Learn about the roles, nature, and works ofGodto more deeply understand Him. Find Scriptures that provide context while revealing His character.

title: Existence of God | History, Philosophy, & Arguments | Britannica
description: Existence ofGod, in religion, the proposition that there is a supreme being that is the creator or sustainer or ruler of the universe and all things in it, including human beings. In many religionsGodis also conceived as perfect, all-powerful and all-knowing, and the source and ultimate ground of morality.

title: God - World History Encyclopedia
description: Learn about the definition, history, and diversity ofGodin different cultures and traditions. Explore the concepts of creation, covenant, prophecy, and monotheism in Judaism, Christianity, Islam, and other faiths.

title: Diocese of Cheyenne - Cheyenne, WY
description: Welcome to the Diocese of Cheyenne We the Catholic Community in the state of Wyoming, proclaim the Gospel of Jesus Christ, celebrate the sacraments of salvation, and invite people into a personal relationship with Christ, through prayer, worship, and service ofGodand neighbor.
